title: Owner Can Exclude Accounts From Fees and Drain Liquidity
reason: `setExcludeFromFee()` is an explicit privileged configuration knob. Fee exemption by itself does not create an unauthorized drain path or a distinct protocol bug under a standard owner-trusted threat model.

title: Owner Can Change Fee Parameters to 0%
reason: False. The external setters enforce `1..25`; `removeAllFee()` is private and only used transiently during fee-exempt transfers.

title: Insufficient Access Control on Manual Send Functions
reason: `manualSend()` is an intended `onlyOwner` treasury forwarding function, not an access-control mistake.

title: No Events Emitted for Critical Parameter Changes
reason: Informational only and not a realistic protocol-impact vulnerability.

title: Missing Validation for Zero Address in setExcludeFromFee
reason: No realistic exploit or protocol harm follows from toggling fee exclusion on `address(0)`.

title: Hardcoded Swap Router Address with No Migration Path
reason: This is a design tradeoff, not a concrete exploitable vulnerability in the deployed logic.

title: receive() Function Accepts Any ETH Without Conditions
reason: Accepting ETH is required for swap proceeds and accidental ETH receipts do not create protocol harm on their own.

title: Lack of Input Validation in includeAccount
reason: No exploit path was substantiated; including an address with zero balance does not create meaningful harm.

title: Token Burns Through Deliver Break Reflection Model
reason: `deliver()` is a standard reflection-token mechanism where callers voluntarily reduce their own reflected balance to benefit other holders; no protocol-breaking flaw is shown.

title: Unlimited Token Mint Through includeAccount
reason: False. `excludeAccount()`/`includeAccount()` only convert between reflected and token-denominated bookkeeping and `includeAccount()` explicitly zeroes `_tOwned[account]`; they do not mint tokens.

title: Owner Can Disable All Transfers Through maxTxAmount
reason: False. `_setMaxTxAmount()` only allows values greater than or equal to `100000000000000e9`, which is above the token's total supply, so the owner cannot lower it to freeze transfers.
